Update the following documentation files. `PlanRef.plan_id` is now `PlanRef.pr_id`. JSON output `"plan_number"` is now `"pr_number"`. The branch `planned-pr-context/{plan_id}` is now `planned-pr-context/{pr_number}`.
IMPORTANT: `state.plan_id`, `PlanContext.plan_id`, `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` are STILL `plan_id` in source. Do NOT rename those.
**File 1: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/objective-update-after-land.md`** - The tripwire warning "Capture plan_id" refers to `state.plan_id` — leave it as `plan_id` - References to `plan_id` that describe state resolution — leave as-is - Only rename `plan_id` if it refers to PlanRef field (unlikely here based on context) - `--plan-number` option → check if this was renamed in CLI to `--pr-number`. If unsure, leave as-is.
**File 2: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/branch-plan-resolution.md`** - `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` — still this name in source, do NOT rename - `PlanNotFound(plan_id=branch_name)` — PlanNotFound may still use `plan_id`, check. It still uses `plan_id` in source. Leave as-is. - `validate_plan_linkage()` — leave as-is - This file mostly documents functions that still use `plan_id`. Likely NO changes needed.
**File 3: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/plan-mismatch-recovery.md`** - `plan_number` in CLI examples like `erk exec setup-impl-from-pr <plan_number>` → check if the CLI arg was renamed. The exec command likely uses `--pr-number` now. Update `<plan_number>` → `<pr_number>` in command examples. - `gh pr view <plan_number>` → `gh pr view <pr_number>` (this is just a placeholder for a number)
**File 4: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-lifecycle.md`** - `plan_id from prepare_state` — this is `state.plan_id`, leave as-is - `plan_number` parameter in `format_plan_next_steps_plain()` — check if this was renamed. If unsure, leave as-is.
**File 5: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md`** - `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` — still this name, leave as-is
**File 6: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md`** - `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` — still this name, leave as-is

**File 8: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/one-shot-workflow.md`** - JSON output: `"plan_number": 1234` → `"pr_number": 1234`
**File 9: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/learn-workflow.md`** - `plan_title` in YAML metadata → `pr_title` - `<learn-plan-number>` placeholder → `<learn-pr-number>`
Read each file first, then apply edits. For files where no changes are needed, skip them.

1→--- 2→title: Objective Update After Land 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the land pipeline's post-merge behavior" 5→ - "working with objective-update-after-land exec script" 6→ - "understanding fail-open patterns in erk" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "making objective-update-after-land exit non-zero" 9→ warning: "This script uses fail-open design. Failures must not block landing. See objective-update-after-land.md." 10→ - action: "resolving plan or objective metadata after the merge pipeline runs" 11→ warning: "Capture plan_id and objective_number BEFORE pipeline execution. The pipeline deletes the branch, making branch-based resolution impossible afterward." 12→ score: 6 13→ - action: "using only branch-based discovery for plan/objective resolution" 14→ warning: "Use direct lookup with --plan parameter when available. Branch-based discovery fails if the branch is already deleted. Direct lookup with fallback is the resilient pattern." 15→ score: 6 16→--- 17→ 18→# Objective Update After Land 19→ 20→After landing a PR, the associated objective issue needs updating. This is handled by the `objective-update-after-land` exec script. 21→ 22→## Location 23→ 24→`src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_update_after_land.py` 25→ 26→## Fail-Open Design 27→ 28→The script always exits 0. The merge has already succeeded at this point — objective update failures must not block the landing workflow. 29→ 30→**Rationale**: The merge is the critical operation; objective tracking is secondary. A failed objective update can always be retried manually.
... [44 lines omitted] ...
38→5. Returns `CommandResult` — handles both success and error cases without raising 67→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) - Related error handling patterns

1→--- 2→title: Branch Plan Resolution 3→read_when: 4→ - "resolving which plan a branch belongs to" 5→ - "working with branch naming conventions for plans" 6→ - "understanding how get_plan_for_branch works" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "assuming branch names always follow the P-prefix format" 9→ warning: "Branch resolution supports multiple formats (P-prefix, objective). Use resolve_plan_id_for_branch() rather than manual parsing. See branch-plan-resolution.md." 10→ - action: "extracting plan ID from branch name instead of using plan-ref.json" 11→ warning: "plan-ref.json is sole source of truth. validate_plan_linkage() only reads plan-ref.json. get_branch_issue() and resolve_plan_id_for_branch() always return None." 12→--- 13→ 14→# Branch Plan Resolution 15→ 16→Given a branch name, resolve which plan it belongs to. 17→ 18→## Methods 19→ 20→These methods live on `PlannedPRBackend` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/planned_pr.py`, accessed via the `PlanBackend` interface. 21→ 22→### `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` 23→ 24→Queries GitHub for a draft PR on the branch. Returns the PR number as a string if found, `None` otherwise. 25→ 26→- Delegates to `GitHub.get_pr_for_branch()` — requires an API call 27→- Returns plan ID as string if a draft PR exists, `None` otherwise 28→- Does NOT fetch full plan content 29→ 30→### `get_plan_for_branch()`
... [30 lines omitted] ...
35→- Returns `Plan | PlanNotFound` discriminated union (not an exception) 44→## Error Types 48→- **`PlanNotFound`**: Frozen dataclass with `plan_id: str`. Returned when a plan can't be found — used as a return type, not an exception (per erk convention). 49→- **`PlanHeaderNotFoundError`**: Exception inheriting from `RuntimeError`. Raised when a plan exists but has no plan-header metadata block. Distinct from "plan not found." 54→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) - Pattern for PlanNotFound return type

1→--- 2→title: Plan Mismatch Recovery 3→read_when: 4→ - "debugging stale plan content in .erk/impl-context/" 5→ - "plan content doesn't match what's on GitHub" 6→ - "re-running implementation setup for an existing plan" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "assuming .erk/impl-context/plan.md always matches the PR body" 9→ warning: "Plan content can become stale after CI updates rewrite the PR body. Re-run setup-impl-from-pr to refresh local plan content." 10→ score: 5 11→--- 12→ 13→# Plan Mismatch Recovery 14→ 15→`.erk/impl-context/plan.md` can diverge from the PR body after CI updates or manual edits. This document covers detection and recovery. 16→ 17→## How Staleness Occurs 18→ 19→1. **CI body rewrites:** `ci_update_pr_body.py` regenerates the PR body with an AI-generated summary, changing the body while the local `plan.md` retains the original content. 20→2. **Manual PR edits:** Editing the PR body on GitHub doesn't propagate to the local `.erk/impl-context/` directory. 21→3. **Re-dispatch:** Running a plan implementation again after the first attempt modified the plan content. 22→ 23→## Recovery: Re-run setup-impl-from-pr 24→ 25→```bash 26→erk exec setup-impl-from-pr <plan_number> 27→``` 28→ 29→This fetches fresh plan content from the PR: 30→
... [38 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Planned PR Lifecycle 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with planned-PR-backed plans" 5→ - "understanding PR body format for planned PR plans" 6→ - "debugging plan content extraction from PR bodies" 7→ - "building or modifying lifecycle stage transitions" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "adding Closes #N to a planned PR footer" 10→ warning: "Planned PR IS the plan. Self-referential close would close the plan itself. Use issue_number=None for github-draft-pr backend." 11→ - action: "adding footer before PR creation" 12→ warning: "PR footer needs the PR number, which isn't known until after create_pr returns. Add footer AFTER PR creation." 13→ - action: "rewriting PR body without preserving metadata" 14→ warning: "Extract metadata prefix on every lifecycle transition via find_metadata_block() to prevent metadata loss." 15→ - action: "parsing plan content without backward compatibility" 16→ warning: "extract_plan_content() handles both details-wrapped and old flat format. Always use it instead of manual parsing." 17→ - action: "using `find_metadata_block` or `extract_plan_content` without validating separator context" 18→ warning: "The content separator `\\n\\n---\\n\\n` can accidentally form from 'Remotely executed' notes + footer delimiter. find_metadata_block() validates via `

1→--- 2→title: Metadata Update Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing plan dispatch metadata updates" 5→ - "choosing between assertive and best-effort metadata operations" 6→ - "working with write_dispatch_metadata or maybe_update_plan_dispatch_metadata" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "using assertive metadata writes in a best-effort context" 9→ warning: "write_dispatch_metadata() raises on error. maybe_update_plan_dispatch_metadata() uses LBYL guards and silent skip with warning. Choose based on whether failure should block the operation." 10→--- 11→ 12→# Metadata Update Patterns 13→ 14→Two contrasting patterns for plan dispatch metadata updates exist in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py`. 15→ 16→## Assertive: `write_dispatch_metadata()` 17→ 18→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py, write_dispatch_metadata --> 19→ 20→Raises `RuntimeError` on any failure. Used when dispatch metadata is a hard requirement. 21→ 22→- Calls `get_workflow_run_node_id()` — raises if None 23→- Calls `get_plan()` — raises if PlanNotFound 24→- Calls `update_metadata()` directly 25→ 26→**Use when:** The caller cannot proceed without metadata being written. 27→ 28→## Best-Effort: `maybe_update_plan_dispatch_metadata()` 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py, maybe_update_plan_dispatch_metadata -->
... [19 lines omitted] ...
32→Uses LBYL guards and returns silently when preconditions aren't met. Prints a warning for partial failures. 37→- Prints dim-styled warning if plan-header is incomplete, then returns

1→--- 2→title: Branch Name Inference 3→last_audited: "2026-02-08 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→tripwires: 6→ - action: "changing branch naming convention (plnd/ prefix)" 7→ warning: "The plnd/ prefix (planned-PR) is a cross-cutting contract used by branch creation, extraction functions, and PR recovery. Changing the prefix format requires updating all consumers. The legacy P{issue}- prefix has been fully removed." 8→ - action: "adding branch_name to plan-header at creation time" 9→ warning: "branch_name is intentionally omitted at creation because the branch doesn't exist yet. The plan-save → branch-create → impl-signal lifecycle requires this gap. See the temporal gap section below." 10→ - action: "recovering a branch name from a PR number using UI or truncated display" 11→ warning: "Use `gh pr view <pr-number> --json headRefName` to recover exact branch names. UI display may truncate long branch names." 12→read_when: 13→ - "debugging missing branch_name in plans" 14→ - "implementing PR lookup from plans" 15→ - "modifying branch creation or naming conventions" 16→--- 17→ 18→# Branch Name Inference 19→ 20→## The Temporal Gap Problem 21→ 22→Plan metadata (`plan-header` block) intentionally omits `branch_name` at creation time because of a temporal gap in the plan lifecycle: 23→ 24→1. **Plan saved** → GitHub issue created (no branch exists yet) 25→2. **Branch created** → User runs `erk br co --for-plan {issue}` (branch now exists) 26→3. **Implementation starts** → `impl-signal started` writes `branch_name` into the plan-header 27→ 28→The gap between steps 1 and 2 is unavoidable: the plan must exist before the branch can be named after it (the branch name contains the issue number). Attempting to set `branch_name` during save would require either predicting the branch name (fragile — the user might choose a custom name) or creating the branch immediately (wrong — the user hasn't started work yet). 29→ 30→## Two-Layer Resolution
... [58 lines omitted] ...
40→If Layer 1 does not produce a match, the command fails with an actionable error. 65→Common failure scenarios and their fixes:

1→--- 2→title: Next Steps Output Formatting 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying next-steps output after plan save or submit" 5→ - "understanding PlanNextSteps" 6→ - "adding new next-steps commands to plan output" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "hardcoding next-steps command strings instead of using the dataclass properties" 9→ warning: "Use PlanNextSteps dataclass from erk_shared.output.next_steps. It is the single source of truth for command formatting." 10→--- 11→ 12→# Next Steps Output Formatting 13→ 14→After saving a plan, erk displays next-steps commands to the user. The formatting is centralized in a single module with one dataclass. 15→ 16→## Source 17→ 18→`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/output/next_steps.py` 19→ 20→## Dataclass 21→ 22→### `PlanNextSteps` 23→ 24→Takes `plan_number: int` and `url: str`. 25→ 26→Properties return pre-formatted command strings. See the source file for the full list of available properties. 27→ 28→## Hierarchical Output Format 29→ 30→The plain-text formatter (`format_plan_next_steps_plain`) produces a hierarchical format with three sections: "Implement plan" (with branch/worktree and dangerous variants), "Checkout plan" (branch/worktree), and "Dispatch plan".
... [63 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: One-Shot Workflow 3→last_audited: "2026-02-24 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "working with erk one-shot dispatch" 7→ - "understanding how plans are autonomously created and implemented" 8→ - "debugging one-shot workflow failures" 9→ - "integrating objectives with one-shot dispatch" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "adding dry-run support to one-shot commands" 12→ warning: "One-shot dry-run mode must NOT create skeleton issues" 13→ - action: "modifying register-one-shot-plan exit behavior" 14→ warning: "register-one-shot-plan uses best-effort: exit 0 if any operation succeeds" 15→ - action: "adding post-dispatch operations without matching dispatch_cmd.py pattern" 16→ warning: "dispatch_one_shot() and _dispatch_planned_pr_plan() in dispatch_cmd.py must stay synchronized. Both use write_dispatch_metadata() + create_submission_queued_block(). Changes to one must be mirrored in the other." 17→ - action: "writing post-dispatch operations without try/except guards" 18→ warning: "Post-dispatch operations (metadata write, queued comment) are best-effort. Wrap in try/except with user-visible warnings. See write_dispatch_metadata() and create_submission_queued_block() in one_shot_dispatch.py." 19→ - action: "editing plan body content in plan creation, replan, or one-shot dispatch" 20→ warning: "One-shot metadata block preservation: the metadata block in the plan body (HTML comment with erk:metadata-block markers) must survive all edits. Never strip or overwrite HTML comment blocks that contain erk:metadata-block markers." 21→ score: 9 22→--- 23→ 24→# One-Shot Workflow 25→ 26→The one-shot workflow enables fully autonomous planning and implementation from a single CLI command. It dispatches a GitHub Actions workflow that creates a plan and implements it without human intervention. 27→ 28→## End-to-End Pipeline 29→ 30→```
... [202 lines omitted] ...
104→`src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py` performs four independent operations that `erk pr submit` normally handles at submit time. Each operation is best-effort -- failures are logged but don't block others: 111→The command outputs JSON results for each operation with success/error details. 124→The CLI write is the primary source; CI registration fills gaps when the CLI couldn't complete (e.g., network failure during dispatch). 213→2. If `rejected: true`, it extracts the `reason` and logs a GitHub Actions warning

1→--- 2→title: Learn Workflow 3→read_when: 4→ - "using /erk:learn skill" 5→ - "understanding learn status tracking" 6→ - "auto-updating parent plans when learn plans land" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "modifying learn command to add/remove/reorder agents" 9→ warning: "Verify tier placement before assigning model. Parallel extraction uses haiku, sequential synthesis may need opus for quality-critical output." 10→ - action: "adding new agents to learn workflow" 11→ warning: "Document input/output format and test file passing. Learn workflow uses stateless agents with file-based composition." 12→ - action: "running /erk:learn in CI" 13→ warning: "CI mode skips interactive prompts and auto-proceeds. Check CI/GITHUB_ACTIONS env vars. See CI Environment Behavior section." 14→ - action: "gathering sessions for preprocessing" 15→ warning: "Sessions >100k characters MUST be preprocessed first. Use erk exec preprocess-session for ~99% token reduction." 16→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 17→audit_result: clean 18→--- 19→ 20→# Learn Workflow 21→ 22→This guide explains the learn workflow in erk: how `/erk:learn` creates documentation plans, tracks status on parent plans, and enables automatic updates when learn plans are landed. 23→ 24→## Overview 25→ 26→The learn workflow extracts insights from implementation sessions and creates documentation plans. It's part of erk's knowledge capture system. 27→ 28→**Key change**: Learn no longer writes documentation directly. Instead, it creates a plan for human review, which is then implemented through the standard `plan-implement` workflow. This gives humans control over documentation quality while maintaining the unified implementation pattern. 29→ 30→```

Update issue title from plan H1 heading 10→ 11→Options: 12→ --plan-number N: Plan number to update (required) 13→ --session-id ID: Session ID to find plan file in scratch storage 14→ --plan-path PATH: Direct path to plan file (overrides session lookup) 15→ 16→Output: 17→ --format json (default): {"success": true, ...} 18→ --format display: Formatted text 19→ 20→Exit Codes: 21→ 0: Success - plan comment updated 22→ 1: Error - plan not found, no plan found, etc. 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk_shared.context.helpers import (

1→"""Update objective after landing a PR. 2→ 3→Standalone exec script that calls Claude to update the linked objective 4→after a PR has been merged. tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: Remote implementations upload session data to planned-pr-context/{plan_id} branches.
Remote implementations upload session data to planned-pr-context/{pr_number} branches.

session.assistant: Let me check what's actually in that section:
session.tool_result:
100→``` 101→ 102→## Registration Step (Best-Effort Composition) 103→ 104→`src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py` performs four independent operations that `erk pr submit` normally handles at submit time. Each operation is best-effort -- failures are logged but don't block others: 105→ 106→1. **Dispatch metadata** -- the primary metadata source is the CLI dispatch (`write_dispatch_metadata()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py`), with CI registration as a fallback. Writes `run_id`, `node_id`, `dispatched_at` to the plan entity's `plan-header` metadata block. 107→2. **Queued comment** -- adds a "Queued for Implementation" emoji comment to the plan with PR link and workflow run URL 108→3. **PR closing reference** -- updates the PR body with `Closes #N` to enable auto-close on merge (handled by PlanBackend) 109→4. **Lifecycle stage** -- updates the plan entity's lifecycle stage to "planned" 110→ 111→The command outputs JSON results for each operation with success/error details. 112→ 113→### PR Closing Reference (Timing Constraint) 114→ 115→The `Closes #N` reference must be in the initial PR body at creation time, not added via a post-creation update. GitHub's `willCloseTarget` behavior is evaluated at PR creation — adding it later does not enable auto-close. The one-shot dispatch handles this by including the closing reference in the initial `create_pr()` call. 116→ 117→### Dispatch Metadata Two-Phase Write 118→ 119→Dispatch metadata is written in two phases: 120→ 121→1. **At dispatch time** (CLI): `write_dispatch_metadata()` writes `dispatched_at`, `run_id`, `node_id` immediately after workflow trigger 122→2. **At registration time** (CI): `register_one_shot_plan.py` writes any remaining fields that weren't available at dispatch time 123→ 124→The CLI write is the primary source; CI registration fills gaps when the CLI couldn't complete (e.g., network failure during dispatch). 125→ 126→### Divergence Risk: One-Shot vs PR Dispatch 127→ 128→One-shot dispatch and `erk pr submit` both push branches and create PRs, but one-shot uses force-push (squash) while plan-submit may use regular push. After a one-shot dispatch, the local branch may diverge from remote. Always `git pull --rebase` before making local changes to a one-shot branch. 129→
... [95 lines omitted] ...
213→2. If `rejected: true`, it extracts the `reason` and logs a GitHub Actions warning

330→ 331→### ExistingDocsChecker 332→ 333→**Input:** 334→ 335→- `plan_title`: Title from plan 336→- `pr_title`: PR title (if available) 337→- `search_hints`: Key terms for searching 338→ 339→**Output:** Report with: 340→ 341→- Existing docs found 342→- Potential duplicates 343→- Contradiction candidates 344→ 345→### DocumentationGapIdentifier 346→ 347→**Input:** 348→ 349→- `session_analysis_paths`: List of session analysis file paths 350→- `diff_analysis_path`: Path to diff analysis (or null) 351→- `existing_docs_path`: Path to existing docs check 352→- `plan_title`: Title from plan 353→ 354→**Output:** Enumerated table with: 355→ 356→- Classification (NEW_DOC, UPDATE_EXISTING, TRIPWIRE, SKIP) 357→- Priority (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) 358→- Deduplication status (ALREADY_DOCUMENTED, PARTIAL_OVERLAP, NEW_TOPIC) 359→
... [58 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→description: Extract insights from plan-associated sessions 3→argument-hint: "[pr-number]" 4→--- 5→ 6→# /erk:learn 7→ 8→Create a documentation plan from Claude Code sessions associated with a plan implementation. The verb "learn" means: analyze what happened, extract insights, and create an actionable plan to document those learnings. 9→ 10→## Usage 11→ 12→``` 13→/erk:learn # Infers plan from current branch 14→/erk:learn 4655 # Explicit PR number 15→``` 16→ 17→## Purpose 18→ 19→All documentation produced by this command follows the content quality standards in the `learned-docs` skill. Load that skill for the full rules on audience (AI agents), the cornerstone principle (cross-cutting insight), and what belongs in learned docs vs code comments. 20→ 21→## Agent Instructions 22→ 23→**Prerequisite:** Load the `learned-docs` skill for content quality standards before proceeding. 24→ 25→Tell the user (choose the appropriate pipeline display based on whether `.erk/impl-context/` exists): 26→ 27→**When `.erk/impl-context/` exists (CI/async mode):** 28→ 29→``` 30→Learn pipeline for PR #<pr-number> (using preprocessed materials):
... [75 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result:
200→```bash 201→mkdir -p .erk/scratch/sessions/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}/learn 202→ 203→# For each local session source with a valid path: 204→# Compare source.session_id to planning_session_id to determine prefix 205→ 206→# For the planning session (source.session_id == planning_session_id): 207→erk exec preprocess-session "<source.path>" \ 208→ --max-tokens 20000 \ 209→ --output-dir .erk/scratch/sessions/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}/learn \ 210→ --prefix planning 211→ 212→# For implementation sessions (source.session_id in implementation_session_ids): 213→erk exec preprocess-session "<source.path>" \ 214→ --max-tokens 20000 \ 215→ --output-dir .erk/scratch/sessions/${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}/learn \ 216→ --prefix impl 217→``` 218→ 219→Note: The preprocessor applies deduplication, truncation, and pruning optimizations automatically. Files are auto-chunked if they exceed the token limit (20000 tokens safely under Claude's 25000 read limit). Output files include session IDs in filenames (e.g., `planning-{session-id}.xml` or `impl-{session-id}-part{N}.xml` for chunked files). 220→ 221→Tell the user: 222→ 223→``` 224→Preprocessing N session(s): compressing JSONL → XML, deduplicating, truncating to 20k tokens each... 225→``` 226→ 227→#### Save PR Comments 228→ 229→If a PR exists for this plan, save PR comments for analysis:
... [255 lines omitted] ...
